msItem.title: Breuis relatio de Guillelmo nobilissimo comite Normannorum
msItem.rubric: Incipit quedam breuis relatio de Willelmo nobilissimo comite Normannorum. quis fuit & unde originem duxit. & quam multitudinem ducens Anglie regnum sibi armis adquisiuit [quam-regnum nearly erased]
msItem.incipit: Pater huius Willelmi fuit Rodbertus dux Normannorum
msItem.note: Van Houts argues that the text is autograph. It was written after 1114 and before 1120; minor revisions were made to this copy after 1120 and before 1135 following the death of William Adelin (ed. Houts, pp. 37, 39).

listBibl.bibl: ed. E. M. C. van Houts, 'The Brevis Relatio de Guillelmo Nobilissimo Comite Normannorum', in Chronology, conquest and conflict in medieval England = Camden miscellany XXXIV, Camden Soc. fifth series 10 (1997).

msItem.title: Treaty between Louis VII of France and Henry II of England, 1177
msItem.incipit: Ego .L. Dei. gratia rex Francorum & ego .H. eadem gratia rex Anglorum
msItem.note: Binding each to a Crusade; printed in Rymer's Foedera (1704), I, 50-1.

msItem.title: Ship list of William the Conqueror
msItem.incipit: Willelmus dux Normannorum ueniens in Angliam
msItem.note: The unique copy. Van Houts dates the script to c. 1130-60 and argues that the the list itself was produced in Normandy, probably at Fécamp, shortly after the Conquest.

listBibl.bibl: Elisabeth M. C. van Houts, ‘The Ship List of William the Conqueror’, in Proceedings of the Battle Conference 1987, ed. by R. Allen Brown, Anglo-Norman Studies, 10 (Boydell, 1988), pp. 159-183, with the text of the list at p. 176.

handDesc.handNote: Item 1 written by a Battle Abbey scribe who also wrote, in addition to fols. 29r-150v of Hereford MS. P.v.1, (1) the autograph manuscript of the annals of Battle Abbey (BL Cotton MS Nero D ii fols. 238-241), (2) BL Royal MS. 4 C xi fols. 1-222r (Houts, 'Ship list', p. 165 and n.). Items 2 and 3 were written by two other scribes.

origin.origDate: 12th century, first half (1114 × 1120 and 1120 × 1135: Houts), with 12th-century additions

history.provenance: Battle, Sussex, Benedictine abbey of St Martin: evidence of contents and script. Formerly one volume with Hereford Cathedral MS. P.v.1. Had apparently left Battle by Leland's time: see CBMLC B9.9.

Brecon, Brecknockshire, Benedictine priory of St John the Evangelist (cell of Battle). According to John Prise, taken to Brecon by a monk of Battle: D. Huws, 'Gildas Prisei', National Library of Wales Journal 17 (1971–2) 314–20.
Sir John Prise (d. 1555): refers to his possession of the manuscript in his Historiae Brytanniae Defensio, 1573, p. 114 etc.
George Allen, succentor and minor canon of Hereford Cathedral (d. 1616), for whom see F. Havergal, Fasti Herefordenses (1869), p. 96 and CCED 168607):
Given by him (as part of Hereford P.v.1) to Hereford Cathedral after 1611 (Houts, 'Ship list', p. 165 n. 39).
The quire that is now MS. e Mus. 93 belonged to Silas Taylor in 1658, for whose dealings with Hereford library see Wood, Athenae, 3. 1175. (MLGB3)
history.acquisition: Given to the Bodleian by Silas Taylor in 1658. He printed the first and third pieces in his History of Gavelkind (Lond. 1663).

recordHist.source:
Adapted by Matthew Holford (March 2026) from the Summary Catalogue (1937) with additional reference to published literature as cited.
Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 2, p. 712

ref.title: MLGB3: Medieval Libraries of Great Britain
MLGB3: Medieval Libraries of Great Britain

revisionDesc.change: Matthew Holford: revised to incorporate all information from Summary Catalogue with reference to published literature.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
